question_text: Geothermal energy is obtained from heat inside the Earth. Explain why geothermal energy is classified as a renewable energy resource.
correct_answer: Geothermal energy is renewable because the heat inside the Earth is continuously produced by radioactive decay in the Earth's core, so it will not run out on a human timescale.
explanation: Renewable energy resources are those that are naturally replenished and will not run out during human lifetimes. Geothermal energy comes from heat deep inside the Earth, which is continuously generated by radioactive elements decaying in the core. Unlike fossil fuels which take millions of years to form, this heat is constantly being produced, making geothermal energy renewable.
